**Land warfare** or **ground warfare** is the process of military operations eventuating in [combat](/wiki/Combat "Combat") that takes place predominantly on the [battlespace](/wiki/Battlespace "Battlespace") land surface of the [planet](/wiki/Earth "Earth").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Land warfare is categorized by the use of large numbers of combat personnel employing a diverse set of combat skills, methods, and a wide variety of weapon systems and equipment, conducted in diverse [terrains](/wiki/Terrain "Terrain") and [weather](/wiki/Weather "Weather") environments. Land warfare, by virtue of being conducted in [defence](/wiki/Defense_(military) "Defense (military)") of urban and rural population areas, dominates the study of [war](/wiki/Military_history "Military history"), and is a focus for most [national defence policy](/wiki/National_defence_policy "National defence policy") planning and financial considerations.

[Land warfare in history](/wiki/Military_history "Military history") has undergone several distinct transitions in conduct from a large concentration of largely untrained and irregularly armed populace used in [frontal assaults](/wiki/Frontal_assault "Frontal assault") to current employment of [combined arms](/wiki/Combined_arms "Combined arms") concepts with highly trained regular troops using a wide variety of organisational, weapon and information systems, and employing a variety of strategic, [operational](/wiki/Operational_doctrine "Operational doctrine") and tactical doctrines.

Although land combat in the past was conducted by the [combat arms](/wiki/Combat_arms "Combat arms") of the [armed forces](/wiki/Armed_forces "Armed forces"), since [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") it has largely involved three distinct types of combat units: [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry"), [armour](/wiki/Armoured_forces "Armoured forces"), and [artillery](/wiki/Artillery "Artillery"). These arms, since the [Age of Sail](/wiki/Age_of_Sail "Age of Sail"), have used [amphibious warfare](/wiki/Amphibious_warfare "Amphibious warfare") concepts and methods to project power from the [seas](/wiki/Sea "Sea") and [oceans](/wiki/Ocean "Ocean"), and since the wide introduction of [military transport aircraft](/wiki/Military_transport_aircraft "Military transport aircraft") and [helicopters](/wiki/Helicopter "Helicopter") have used [airborne forces](/wiki/Airborne_forces "Airborne forces") and [vertical envelopment](/wiki/Vertical_envelopment "Vertical envelopment") to the variety of [doctrines](/wiki/Military_doctrine "Military doctrine") used to prosecute warfare on land.

## Land forces

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Land forces")]

[Land forces](/wiki/Land_forces "Land forces") include [personnel](/wiki/Personnel "Personnel"), [weapons platforms](/wiki/Weapons_platform "Weapons platform"), [vehicles](/wiki/Vehicles "Vehicles"), and support elements operating on land to accomplish assigned missions and tasks.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Infantry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Infantry")]

Main article: [Infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry")

Infantry are [soldiers](/wiki/Soldiers "Soldiers") who fight primarily on foot with [small arms](/wiki/Small_arms "Small arms") in organized [military units](/wiki/Military_unit "Military unit"). However, they may be transported to the battlefield by [ships](/wiki/Ship "Ship"), [automobiles](/wiki/Automobile "Automobile"), [skis](/wiki/Ski "Ski"), cargo planes, or other means.

### Combat vehicles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Combat vehicles")]

Main article: [Combat vehicle](/wiki/Combat_vehicle "Combat vehicle")

[Combat vehicles](/wiki/Combat_vehicle "Combat vehicle") provide the means to mobilize heavy firepower to engage opposing forces including other combat vehicles. Combat vehicles are usually equipped to drive in rugged [terrain](/wiki/Terrain "Terrain"). They are usually protected against other common threats with [armor](/wiki/Vehicle_armour "Vehicle armour") and other [countermeasures](/wiki/Countermeasure "Countermeasure").

Examples of combat vehicles include [main battle tanks](/wiki/Main_battle_tank "Main battle tank"), [infantry fighting vehicles](/wiki/Infantry_fighting_vehicle "Infantry fighting vehicle"), and [self-propelled artillery](/wiki/Self-propelled_artillery "Self-propelled artillery").

### Artillery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Artillery")]

Main article: [Artillery](/wiki/Artillery "Artillery")

Historically, artillery (from French artillerie) refers to any engine used for the discharge of projectiles during the war. The term also describes ground-based troops, who primarily manned such weapons. The word is derived from the Old French verb attilier, meaning "to equip".

This term includes coastal artillery which traditionally defended coastal areas against seaborne attack and controlled the passage of ships using their ability to deny access through the threat of coastal fire. It also includes land-based field artillery. With the advent of powered flight at the start of the 20th century, artillery also included ground-based anti-aircraft batteries.

## Combined arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Combined arms")]

Main article: [Combined arms](/wiki/Combined_arms "Combined arms")

[Combined arms](/wiki/Combined_arms "Combined arms") is an approach to [warfare](/wiki/War "War") which seeks to integrate different arms of a [military](/wiki/Military "Military") to achieve mutually complementary effects, such as, [self-propelled artillery](/wiki/Self-propelled_artillery "Self-propelled artillery"), [mechanized infantry](/wiki/Mechanized_infantry "Mechanized infantry"), [aircraft](/wiki/Aircraft "Aircraft") and so forth.
